case_name: Bon Secours-DePaul Medical Center v. Rogakos-Russell

summary.brief: Bon Secours-DePaul Medical Center v. Rogakos-Russell. Medical malpractice case involving neurology.

evidence_index.text: Father Constantine P. Rogakos, a retired Greek-Orthodox priest, checked in at Bon Secours hospital for an outpatient abdominal ultrasound.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: He walked with a 'quad' cane. His wife described him as having a 'shuffled gait.'
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Regan informed Father Rogakos that he should 'remove [his] clothing from the waist up' and put on a medical gown that was laid out for him on a wheeled hospital stretcher.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Regan left the room while Father Rogakos changed.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: He suffered a laceration on his head, a right periorbital hematoma, and an intracranial hemorrhage.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: After ten days, his family decided to withdraw life support, and he passed away.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Two experts testified for the Plaintiff concerning the standard of care. They opined that the standard of care required Hospital personnel to assess Father Rogakos for the risk that he might fall.
evidence_index.speaker: Plaintiff's experts

evidence_index.text: The Hospital’s standard of care experts testified that the Hospital complied with the standard of care in leaving Father Rogakos alone in the room to change.
evidence_index.speaker: Hospital's experts

evidence_index.text: He stated that he leaned on the wheeled hospital stretcher while he was changing, the stretcher moved, and he lost his balance and fell.
evidence_index.speaker: Father Rogakos

evidence_index.text: The judgment of the Court of Appeals is affirmed.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The jury could properly conclude on this evidence that Father Rogakos’ fall would not have occurred absent the Hospital’s negligence.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

patient_demographics.relevant_social_history: Retired Greek-Orthodox priest, uses a quad cane, shuffled gait

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Father Constantine P. Rogakos presented for an outpatient abdominal ultrasound to diagnose bloating and abdominal pain.

focused_exam.general: Walks with a quad cane, shuffled gait

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Abdominal pain - differential includes gastrointestinal issues

requestables.name: Abdominal Ultrasound
requestables.description: Ultrasound to assess abdominal pain and bloating

timeline_phases.name: Hospital Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient arrives at hospital for scheduled ultrasound

key_events.event: Patient checked in and taken to ultrasound room

timeline_phases.name: Ultrasound Preparation
timeline_phases.description: Patient instructed to change into a gown

key_events.event: Patient left alone to change, fall occurs

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in ultrasound room, instructed to change into a gown
decision_points.prompt: What precautions should be taken to ensure patient safety while changing?

options.description: Ensure stretcher wheels are locked and assist patient while changing
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard care for fall risk patients

options.description: Leave patient alone to change without checking stretcher wheels

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant left patient alone without checking stretcher wheels
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant did not perceive patient as needing assistance

expected_action_court.description: Ensure stretcher wheels are locked and assist patient
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony on fall risk management

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to assess and mitigate fall risk
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Patient should have been assisted and stretcher secured

patient_outcome.description: Patient suffered a fall resulting in a head injury and intracranial hemorrhage
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Patient passed away after life support was withdrawn

key_findings: Failure to monitor and assist patient
Failure to ensure stretcher wheels were locked

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to ensure patient safety during changing
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Assistance and secured stretcher would have prevented fall

factual_timeline.event: Patient arrives at hospital for ultrasound

allegations.allegation: Failure to monitor and assist patient
